key_highlights: U.S. stocks hit record highs on strong jobs data and chipmaker surge, with S&P 500 closing out 6th straight week of gains.
Oil prices volatile: report of U.S.-Iran deal caused a plunge, but later rose after exchange of fire in Hormuz strait; DOJ investigating suspicious oil trades.
Iran war continues to disrupt global supply chains: China energy imports plunge, Africa fertilizer crisis, Air India cuts costs and flights.
Nvidia remains a central theme: funds Corning plants, partners with IREN for AI deployments, but also faces chip smuggling allegations via Thailand.
IPO market active: BlackRock launches tokenized money-market funds, Safepoint, Applied Aerospace, Quantinuum, HawkEye 360, Inspire Brands file or debut.
Fed policy expectations shift: Goldman sees cuts delayed to December, Boston Fed president discusses dissent; Treasuries gain on inflation focus.
Mortgage rates remain elevated due to Iran uncertainty, but money market and CD rates offer up to 4.1% APY.
Healthcare sector mixed: UnitedHealth eliminates prior authorization for 30% of services, LifeStance Health jumps 20%, but Odyssey and Mobia fall after IPO.
Geopolitical shifts: Suvendu Adhikari becomes first BJP CM of West Bengal, Pakistan gets IMF tranche, Venezuela lands oil deals.
Ted Turner, CNN founder, dies at 87, marking end of an era in media.

sectors.trend: Strong bullish momentum driven by AI demand, Nvidia partnerships, and record highs in chip stocks.
sectors.implications: Nvidia's investments in Corning and IREN signal long-term AI infrastructure buildout. However, chip smuggling allegations and regulatory scrutiny (DOJ, White House distancing from AI regulation) add risk.

sectors.trend: Extreme volatility due to Iran war: prices plunged on deal rumors, then spiked on Hormuz strait clashes. DOJ investigating suspicious trades.
sectors.implications: Energy sector remains high-risk, high-reward. Occidental Petroleum beats guidance, but supply disruptions (China imports plunge, Africa fertilizer crisis) create both opportunities and risks.

sectors.trend: Mixed: Deutsche Bank upgrades DaVita, but lowers Capital One targets. Fed sees private credit redemptions as manageable. Goldman delays rate cut expectations.
sectors.implications: Banks face margin pressure from delayed Fed cuts, but IPO activity (BlackRock tokenized funds, Safepoint, etc.) and private credit resilience provide some support.

sectors.trend: Divergent: UnitedHealth reduces prior authorization (positive for patients), LifeStance Health surges 20%, but Odyssey and Mobia fall post-IPO. Elder care is a bright spot.
sectors.implications: Regulatory easing and demographic trends support healthcare services, but IPO performance suggests selective investor appetite.

sectors.trend: Mortgage rates remain elevated due to Iran uncertainty, but HELOC rates are stable. Warren Buffett sends blunt message on home financing.
sectors.implications: High rates continue to pressure housing affordability and refinancing activity. However, stable HELOC rates may support home equity borrowing.

sectors.trend: Applied Aerospace files for IPO amid pre-SpaceX rush, HawkEye 360 stock surges in debut. Drone stocks expected to outperform this summer.
sectors.implications: Geopolitical tensions and space commercialization drive interest in defense and aerospace IPOs. Drone stocks benefit from military and commercial demand.

key_themes: Geopolitical risk (Iran war) driving oil volatility and supply chain disruptions
AI boom fueling semiconductor and infrastructure investments
IPO market revival across multiple sectors (fintech, aerospace, healthcare, consumer)
Fed policy uncertainty and delayed rate cuts
Regulatory scrutiny on AI, chip exports, and oil trading
Consumer staples and value stocks gaining attention (Kraft Heinz, Chevron, American Express)
Cybersecurity threats (Mythos, school software hack) prompting urgent action
Tokenization and crypto-backed mortgages entering mainstream finance

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Iran War Escalation / Oil Supply Disruption
risk_assessment.mitigation: Diversify energy exposure, consider long positions in U.S. energy producers (OXY) and short-term hedges via oil futures or ETFs.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: DOJ Investigation into Suspicious Oil Trades
risk_assessment.mitigation: Avoid speculative trading in oil futures and prediction markets; monitor regulatory developments for potential market manipulation penalties.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Fed Policy Uncertainty / Delayed Rate Cuts
risk_assessment.mitigation: Favor short-duration bonds, floating-rate notes, and value stocks with strong cash flows; reduce exposure to rate-sensitive sectors like real estate.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Chip Export Controls / Nvidia Smuggling Allegations
risk_assessment.mitigation: Monitor U.S.-China trade tensions; Nvidia remains a core AI play but consider hedging with semiconductor ETFs or diversifying into non-exposed chipmakers.
Increase allocation to cybersecurity stocks (e.g., Cloudflare despite layoffs) and ensure portfolio companies have robust cyber protocols.
TIPS, commodities, and value stocks with pricing power (Kraft Heinz, Nucor) can hedge against inflation; avoid long-duration bonds.
Selectively participate in IPOs with strong fundamentals (HawkEye 360, Quantinuum); avoid chasing hype in pre-revenue companies.

opportunities.recommendation: Increase exposure to AI and semiconductor infrastructure plays
opportunities.rationale: Nvidia's investments in Corning and IREN, plus record chip stock highs, indicate sustained AI demand. Partnerships and capacity expansion drive long-term growth.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy U.S. energy producers on pullbacks
opportunities.rationale: Iran war disruption supports oil prices; Occidental Petroleum beat guidance. Supply constraints and geopolitical risk premium favor domestic producers.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in cybersecurity and defense stocks
opportunities.rationale: Rising cyber threats (Mythos, school hack) and geopolitical tensions drive demand. HawkEye 360 and drone stocks are well-positioned.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider tokenized money-market funds for yield
opportunities.rationale: BlackRock's launch signals institutional adoption; yields up to 4.1% APY with blockchain efficiency. Suitable for cash allocation.

opportunities.recommendation: Accumulate value stocks with strong fundamentals
opportunities.rationale: Warren Buffett favorites (Chevron, American Express) and upgraded names (DaVita, Nucor) offer resilience amid uncertainty and inflation hedging.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to oil futures and prediction markets
defensive_moves.rationale: DOJ investigation into suspicious trades and extreme volatility create regulatory and manipulation risks. Stick to physical equities or ETFs.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge against rate hike delays with short-duration bonds
defensive_moves.rationale: Goldman sees Fed cuts delayed to December; Treasuries gained on inflation focus. Short-duration TIPS or floating-rate notes reduce duration risk.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Diversify away from China-exposed semiconductor stocks
defensive_moves.rationale: Nvidia smuggling allegations and White House distancing from AI regulation increase regulatory risk. Consider non-China-exposed chipmakers.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Maintain cash or money market positions for liquidity
defensive_moves.rationale: Elevated rates (up to 4.1% APY) provide attractive risk-free returns while waiting for clearer Fed and geopolitical signals.

market_outlook.short_term: 1-3 month outlook: Markets likely to remain volatile due to Iran war developments, Fed policy uncertainty, and earnings season. Record highs in stocks may face headwinds from oil price spikes and inflation concerns. Defensive sectors and value stocks should outperform growth.
market_outlook.long_term: 6-12 month outlook: AI-driven productivity gains and infrastructure spending support structural growth. However, geopolitical fragmentation (Iran, U.S.-China) and delayed rate cuts could cap upside. Diversification across AI, energy, and value is key.
key_catalysts: Fed meeting in June and inflation data (CPI, PCE)
Nvidia earnings and AI capex announcements
U.S. presidential election cycle and regulatory changes
IPO pipeline (BlackRock tokenized funds, Inspire Brands, Quantinuum)
DOJ investigation outcomes on oil trading
China economic stimulus and energy import recovery
watch_list: Oil prices (WTI, Brent) and Hormuz strait shipping disruptions
10-year Treasury yield and Fed funds futures
Nvidia (NVDA) stock price and Corning (GLW) partnership progress
U.S. dollar index and emerging market currencies
Cybersecurity breach reports and regulatory responses
Mortgage rates and housing market data
IPO aftermarket performance (HawkEye 360, Safepoint, Applied Aerospace)
India political developments (West Bengal CM change)
Canada Post funding and labor stability
Private credit market redemptions and Fed oversight
